headline: Chip Surge Powers Broad Advance Even as Gulf War Clouds Gather
body_html:
Semiconductor stocks are delivering an outsized jolt to Thursday's mid-session tape, with the sector running ahead by more than five-and-a-half percent and pulling the broader market firmly into positive territory. Advancing issues outnumber decliners by a commanding 244-to-146 margin across the 394-name universe, a breadth reading that suggests the rally carries real conviction rather than narrow index arithmetic. Homebuilders, Materials, and Industrials are also holding modest gains, while Energy is the lone sector flashing red in meaningful fashion.

The standout mover of the day is Onto Innovation, the semiconductor-equipment specialist, up nearly fourteen percent mid-session. It is joined by KLA Corp, Arm Holdings, and Lam Research — each adding more than ten percent — alongside Applied Materials, which is not far behind. The common thread is a wave of enthusiasm for chip-related names that appears to be overriding anxiety about escalating hostilities in the Gulf, where fresh attacks on U.S. military assets have rattled currency and oil markets simultaneously.

Into the close, traders will be watching whether that geopolitical overhang reasserts itself. Oil is rising on dimming peace prospects, the dollar is elevated on safe-haven flows and revived Federal Reserve rate-hike speculation, and gold is firming modestly. Any fresh headline out of the Middle East could test how durable the semiconductor-led advance truly is.

briefs.headline: Onto Innovation Leads All Gainers With Explosive Mid-Session Move
briefs.body: Onto Innovation is surging nearly fourteen percent in Thursday trading, making it the session's most dramatic mover. The semiconductor-equipment name is riding the same wave lifting peers KLA Corp, Lam Research, and Applied Materials, all of which are posting double-digit percentage advances as chip-sector enthusiasm dominates the tape.

briefs.headline: Iran Strikes U.S. Military Targets in Gulf, Rattling Oil and Currency Markets
briefs.body: Fresh hostilities in the Gulf — with Iran reported to have struck U.S. military positions — are keeping oil prices elevated and the dollar strong. Safe-haven demand and renewed speculation about Federal Reserve tightening are running in parallel, creating a complicated macro backdrop even as equities push higher on the semiconductor surge.

briefs.headline: Alnylam Biotech Shares Jump More Than Twelve Percent
briefs.body: Alnylam Pharmaceuticals is the session's most prominent non-semiconductor gainer, climbing more than twelve and a half percent. The pharma-biotech name stands out in a Healthcare sector that is collectively up only half a percent, suggesting a company-specific catalyst is driving the outsized move.

briefs.headline: Energy Sector Drags as Oil-Linked Equities Diverge From Crude
briefs.body: Despite rising oil prices tied to Gulf conflict uncertainty, the Energy sector is among Thursday's underperformers, off nearly half a percent. Chord Energy and Murphy Oil are each declining more than two percent mid-session, a divergence that may reflect investor concerns about the broader economic toll of a prolonged Middle East conflict rather than near-term crude dynamics.

briefs.headline: Salesforce Weighs on Software as Sector Posts Only Modest Gain
briefs.body: The Software sector is clinging to a fractional advance of roughly a quarter percent, weighed down in part by a decline of more than two and a half percent in Salesforce shares. The weakness in the enterprise-software bellwether stands in sharp contrast to the enthusiasm driving chip-related names on the same session.

briefs.headline: Bank of Japan Flags Inflation Risk From Iran War as Asian Markets Wobble
briefs.body: The Bank of Japan has signaled that the Iran conflict is stoking domestic inflation pressures, adding a fresh dimension to the geopolitical stress rippling through global markets. Asian equities wobbled overnight as oil jumped on renewed Gulf hostilities, and gold ticked higher on a slightly softer dollar even as the greenback remains broadly elevated.
